what_it_does: A browser-side IIFE registry that maps semantic surface zone identifiers to CSS custom-property names for background, edge, and accent colors, and exposes helpers to retrieve var() strings, inline style objects, and resolved computed values.
observations: Defines window.MS_SURFACES as an IIFE with a private ZONES dictionary.
ZONES contains 10 semantic zones: base, content, panel, review, source, package, success, warning, rejected, vi.
Each zone stores label, purpose, bg, edge, and accent CSS custom property names.
Uses a magenta sentinel (#ff00ff) and console.error for unknown zones or parts.
Provides v(), bg(), edge(), accent(), style(), value(), zone(), has(), names(), and exposes _zones.
value() reads live computed style from document.documentElement.
style() returns a JS style object with optional border and text color.
Comments document a design rationale: avoid undifferentiated gold/cream and reserve gold tint for the vi decision zone.
standouts: Semantic surface zoning tied to functional roles rather than literal colors.
No-silent-fallback sentinel pattern with explicit console.error.
CSS-variable indirection keeps actual tint values in the stylesheet.
value() resolves live computed style for canvas or measuring use cases.
conventions: IIFE module pattern attached to a window global.
References CSS custom properties by string names and wraps them in var().
Defensive lookup with sentinel return and error logging.
Extensive header comments explaining design intent.
concerns: value() requires a live DOM and document.documentElement, so it is unsuitable outside a browser environment.
_zones is exposed directly, allowing external mutation of the registry.
No validation that the referenced CSS custom properties are actually defined.
Hard-coded sentinel string is not configurable.
notes: A semantic surface token registry that keeps JavaScript color references decoupled from CSS values and loudly fails on misuse.
questions: Are the referenced CSS custom properties defined in colors_and_type.css as the header claims?
Is there a plan to migrate this global IIFE to an ES module?
How should production UI behave when a zone lookup returns the magenta sentinel?
Which canvas or measuring consumers rely on value()?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it is a typed registry for referencing semantic surface zones via CSS custom properties and provides accessor helpers.
apparent_themes: Design-token separation of values from references
Restrained accent-color usage to preserve meaning
intention_signals: The file is trying to enforce a disciplined semantic surface system where every functional zone has a consistent near-white tinted wash and gold is reserved only for active decision/Vi surfaces, preventing the prior drift toward undifferentiated cream backgrounds.
novelty: The combination of an explicit no-silent-fallback sentinel, semantic zone roles, and CSS-variable-only references, together with embedded design-rationale comments in a token registry.
contribution: Provides a stable, named interface for JavaScript to request surface colors by semantic role without hard-coding color values, and encodes the studio's zoning policy so downstream surfaces can reference it consistently.
summary_for_embedding: surfaces.js is a browser IIFE that registers semantic surface zones and exposes helpers to retrieve CSS custom-property references, inline style objects, and computed color values for each zone.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to be the single source of truth for referencing semantic surface tints from JavaScript while keeping actual color values in CSS.

symbols.does: Looks up a zone by id and returns its descriptor, logging an error and returning null for unknown zones.
symbols.description: spec is the internal lookup function for the ZONES registry. It validates the requested zone id, emits a console.error listing known zones if the id is missing, and returns null so callers can short-circuit rather than silently fall back.

symbols.does: Returns a CSS var() string for a given zone and part (bg, edge, or accent).
symbols.description: v resolves a zone descriptor via spec and wraps the requested CSS custom property name in a var(...) string. If the zone or part is unknown it returns the magenta sentinel and logs an error.

symbols.does: Convenience wrapper that returns the var() reference for a zone's background custom property.
symbols.description: bg calls v(id, 'bg'), providing a short way to request the surface background variable for a semantic zone.

symbols.does: Convenience wrapper that returns the var() reference for a zone's edge/border custom property.
symbols.description: edge calls v(id, 'edge'), providing a short way to request the surface border variable for a semantic zone.

symbols.does: Convenience wrapper that returns the var() reference for a zone's accent/ink custom property.
symbols.description: accent calls v(id, 'accent'), providing a short way to request the zone's text or accent color variable.

symbols.does: Builds an inline style object with background and optional border/color for a zone.
symbols.description: style returns a JavaScript style object using the zone's background variable, optionally adding a 1px solid edge border and setting the accent as the text color. It respects opts.border === false and opts.ink.

symbols.does: Resolves the literal computed value of a zone's CSS custom property from the document root.
symbols.description: value reads the current computed value of the requested custom property from document.documentElement, useful for canvas drawing or measuring, and falls back to the magenta sentinel if the property is empty or missing.

symbols.does: Returns a zone descriptor augmented with its id, or null if unknown.
symbols.description: zone looks up a zone via spec and, if found, returns a shallow copy of the descriptor with the id property merged in, making it easier for consumers to iterate or display zone metadata.
